# confluent_switchover_pair Resource

[![General Availability](https://img.shields.io/badge/Lifecycle%20Stage-General%20Availability-%2345c6e8)](https://docs.confluent.io/cloud/current/api.html#section/Versioning/API-Lifecycle-Policy)

`confluent_switchover_pair` provides a SwitchoverPair resource that enables creating, editing, and deleting SwitchoverPairs on Confluent Cloud.

-> **Note:** It is recommended to set `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` on production instances to prevent accidental switchover pair deletion. This setting rejects plans that would destroy or recreate the switchover pair, such as attempting to change uneditable attributes. Read more about it in the [Terraform docs](https://www.terraform.io/language/meta-arguments/lifecycle#prevent_destroy).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "confluent_switchover_pair" "example" {
  active_member = "west"
  display_name = "prod-kafka-dr"
  environment {
    id = "env-00000"
  }
  members = ""
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


- `active_member` - (Required String) The name of the member that is currently active. On create, this selects which member starts as active; it must match one of the `members[].name` values. Use the `:failover` operation to change the active member after creation.
- `display_name` - (Required String) A human-readable name for the switchover pair.
- `environment` - (Required Configuration Block) The environment to which this belongs. Supports the following:
    - `id` - (Required String) The ID of the Environment.
- `members` - (Required String) The two clusters participating in this switchover pair. Must contain exactly 2 members.

## Attributes Reference

In addition to the preceding arguments, the following attributes are exported:


- `api_version` - (Required String) API Version defines the schema version of this representation of a resource.
- `kind` - (Required String) Kind defines the object this REST resource represents.
- `resource_name` - (Required String) The Confluent Resource Name of the resource.

## Import

-> **Note:** `CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_KEY` and `CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_SECRET` environment variables must be set before importing a SwitchoverPair.

You can import a SwitchoverPair by using the composite ID `<Environment ID>/<SwitchoverPair ID>`, for example:

```shell
$ export CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_KEY="<cloud_api_key>"
$ export CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_SECRET="<cloud_api_secret>"
$ terraform import confluent_switchover_pair.example env-abc123/swi-abc123
```

!> **Warning:** Do not forget to delete terminal command history afterwards for security purposes.
